claims.claim_text: Tomato was successfully grown after rice in upland rice fallows within an upland rice-based cropping system; tomato varieties performed well when planted early (16 November) with 15-day-old seedlings, with fruit yields up to 60.7 t/ha.
claims.evidence_text: A study was conducted during the winter season of 1994-95 and 1995-96 in upland rice (Ofyza sativa L.) fallows to evaluate the impact of seedling age and planting time on the fruit yield performance of different varieties ('BT la', 'BT 12', 'BT 1 O', 'BT 2' and 'MIX ENT') of tomato (Lycopersicon lycopersicum Mill.).

context.system: upland rice-based cropping system / rice fallows

provenance.source_title:
Impact of seedling age and planting time on yield performance of tomato (Lycopersicon lycopersicum) in upland rice (Oryza sativa)-based cropping system
							| Indian Journal of Agronomy
